item.title: Topolski's Chronicle, V.7 No.3-4, 1959
item.creator: Topolski, Feliks, 1907-1989
item.description: Topolski's Chronicle is a series of broadsheets created by Feliks Topolski and distributed bimonthly to subscribers from 1953 through 1982. Topolski was a superlative draughtsman and covered current events with an artistic, unique style.

pages.typeset_text:
TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE No. 3-4 (135-136) Vol VII 1959
UNIVERSITY OF UTAH LIBRARIES

THE DOUBLE BASS COATS IN SEPIA - 'TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE' IS PUBLISHED IN ENGLAND AT 35 SHOE LANE, E.C.4. COPY YEARLY SUBSCRIPTION RATES (POSTAGE PAID UK) 16/.- ALSO ISSUED ON THE PLACE BY THE SCHOOL AND AT 35 SHOE LANE, LONDON. PRINTED BY RIGDEN'S, THE NEWS AND VINTNERS. ESSEX HOUSE 1957/58. THE ABOVE TITLE AND DATE ARE HELD THAT THIS PRODUCER THE ENSLAVED CONSTITUTION BELONGS TO FELIX TOFOLSKI AND THE CHRONICLE APPEARS FOR 35/6 EACH ISSUE
pages.handwritten_text:
Dancer sports his rank - stripes of police sergeant

people may live on the West Coast but they mightn't want a glorious memorial to the mosquito - The chief, plastic for West African Mosquitoes. Roughness East and South have their Dominance in the West. Draws from a freind [?]
sketch_descriptions: Upper sketch showing three abstract, dynamic figures with fluid, elongated limbs and exaggerated postures, one figure holding what appears to be a drum or shield, captioned as a dancer wearing police sergeant stripes.

Lower sketch depicting a group of slender, simplified human figures in various poses, some wearing hats and traditional attire, with one figure holding a long stick or spear, suggesting a scene of African people possibly on the West Coast.
pages.page_summary: This page from Topolski's Chronicle features two expressive sketches: one of a dancer wearing police sergeant stripes and another depicting figures possibly representing West African people and their interaction with mosquito-related challenges, accompanied by handwritten notes and editorial typeset text.
